preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Startups seeking an AI-first, intent-driven design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context for startup audience evaluating MODE as their AI-first design system. At the decision stage, visitors already know the product — this beat reaffirms who it's for and frames the page as the final step from trial to paid.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators need trust signals before they commit. Placing credibility early — startup logos, testimonials, partner badges — reassures decision-stage visitors that peers have already validated MODE, reducing perceived risk of upgrading.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required by both the decision funnel stage and the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study results, and ROI data from real startups give the proof-driven Validator the hard evidence they need to justify the purchase internally.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents clear plan comparison and pricing so trial users can evaluate exactly what they gain by upgrading. Positioned after evidence so the value-to-cost equation feels obvious.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: The commitment prompt — upgrade CTA with urgency framing. Placed immediately before Recovery per rules, and directly after Decision so the action step follows naturally from plan selection.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths — FAQ links, contact sales, feature comparison docs — for Validators who need one more piece of reassurance before converting, ensuring no visitor exits without an alternative route.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes context for startup audience evaluating MODE as their AI-first design system. At the decision stage, visitors already know the product — this beat reaffirms who it's for and frames the page as the final step from trial to paid.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators need trust signals before they commit. Placing credibility early — startup logos, testimonials, partner badges — reassures decision-stage visitors that peers have already validated MODE, reducing perceived risk of upgrading.

sections.name: Proof points
sections.rationale: Required by both the decision funnel stage and the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study results, and ROI data from real startups give the proof-driven Validator the hard evidence they need to justify the purchase internally.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents clear plan comparison and pricing so trial users can evaluate exactly what they gain by upgrading. Positioned after evidence so the value-to-cost equation feels obvious.

sections.name: CTA
sections.rationale: The commitment prompt — upgrade CTA with urgency framing. Placed immediately before Recovery per rules, and directly after Decision so the action step follows naturally from plan selection.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages — never suppress it.
For conversion stage use text-only: slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.

slots.headline: Your trial worked. Here's what you're keeping.
slots.subhead: MODE resolves component behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register to the right experience for every archetype and funnel stage — without manual variants or QA overhead.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. No subscription.

page.reasoning: Validator variant always includes trust_signal. Always include role and company for Validator credibility — with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity. Place near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.

slots.quote: We cut our design-to-code review cycle from three days to four hours. MODE doesn't just generate variants — it makes the right call before anyone has to review it.

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust.

slots.headline: What teams measure after shipping MODE

stats.label: Median time from install to first resolved output
Faster page architecture from brief to component plan
Manual variant files maintained after adopting the resolution engine

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan — use sparingly (one per pricing section).

slots.description: The full intelligence layer — resolution engine, component manifest, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. Yours forever, no subscription.
features: Three palette presets, editable token dashboard
Next.js preview UI with palette and label overlays
Bring your own Anthropic API key — you own the spend

page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required').

slots.headline: The semantic layer your design system is missing.
slots.subhead: MODE lives in your codebase, resolves behavior without configuration, and ships in under an hour. The decision is already made — this is the step.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. No subscription. No runtime dependency.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.
